title: Gawd First-Ship Validation Suite — Result Schema
description: Schema for the JSON result emitted by `gawd-validate first-ship`. Produced by F3 (METATRON 2026-05-26). Consumed by F2 (graduation-gate.sh), F1 (gawd-import.sh self-test), and on-demand operator invocations.

version.description: Schema version. Bumped on any breaking change.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at start of suite run.

workspace.description: Absolute path to the Gawd workspace under test.

duration_sec.description: Wall-clock seconds the suite took to run end-to-end.

status.description: Aggregate verdict for this validation. `tool-broken` means the primitive itself errored (exit 2).

primitive_owner.description: The upstream handoff that produced the primitive this validation wraps. Failure diagnosis routes here.

primitive_path.description: Absolute path to the primitive script invoked.

exit_code.description: Exit code returned by the primitive.

diagnostic_message.description: Human-readable summary of failure (empty on pass). Cites primitive_owner so operators know where to diagnose.

sub_assertions.description: Per-assertion detail forwarded from the primitive (e.g., B2's three meeting assertions, F1's three T0 assertions).

primitive_result_file.description: Path to the primitive's own result JSON, if it wrote one. For deep-diagnosis.

stub_mode.description: True if this validation ran against a stubbed primitive (e.g., E3 before it lands). When true, status MUST be `skip` and operators MUST treat the overall verdict as conditional.

verdict.description: Aggregate verdict. `pass` if all validations pass; `fail` if any fail or tool-broken; `conditional` if any are stubbed/skipped but no fails — F2 should treat conditional as PASS-with-warning, not as PASS.
